description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly beige to light tan coloration. The surface features a mix of smooth and jagged textures, with some areas appearing slightly polished and others rougher, possibly due to natural weathering. Subtle gray and bluish streaks run through parts of the rock, adding visual interest and suggesting mineral inclusions. One side displays a more pronounced ridge and a sloped face, while the opposite side is flatter and broader. There is a visible marking or inscription on one face, indicating possible cataloging or human handling. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, beige-tan rock with gray streaks, angular surfaces, and visible markings, suitable as a natural barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the rock with visible surface markings and subtle color variations.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rock's jagged, angular peak and the transition from beige to bluish-gray tones.
description_view_2: The side profile reveals the elongated, blocky shape and layered texture, with muted color bands.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the pointed ridge and the darker, more pronounced gray streaks on the rock's surface.
